behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff/orchestration: dispatcher must fan out one document plus role-specific instruction packs to three reviewer clients. A unit test of one helper would miss wiring mistakes (wrong role gets wrong pack). Rejected System because behavior text is about payload routing correctness, not real network/OS/subprocess behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because correctness here does not depend on model judgment, only request construction and fanout.
behaviors.test_description: Run dispatch orchestration in-process with three fake reviewer adapters; assert each adapter receives identical document bytes and its own expected instruction pack, with no cross-role mixups.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly evaluates extraction quality from a live AI reviewer. Mocked/stubbed outputs would hide real model misses on legal language. Rejected Workflow because this behavior isolates a single reviewer decision, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real clause extractor on representative real contracts and assert required term categories are correctly identified with acceptable recall/precision thresholds.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is invocation wiring/config propagation between modules. Real model output is not needed to catch wrong policy payload shape/values. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is about 'configuration is passed correctly,' not whether model policy interpretation is good.
behaviors.test_description: Execute risk-assessor invocation path with a fake assessor endpoint/client and assert outbound request contains exact policy config (version, thresholds, enabled rules) expected by contract.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Detection of indirect/euphemistic problematic language is model judgment quality, inherently AI-output dependent. Stubs cannot reveal false negatives/positives from real phrasing nuance. Rejected Workflow because this behavior targets one reviewer’s decision quality, not chained agent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real risk assessor on curated clauses containing direct and euphemistic risky language; verify it flags policy violations at target quality thresholds.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: Failure occurs at local handoff from orchestration/config layers into compliance-invocation payload. No real OS/network behavior required to detect mis-mapped jurisdiction/profile fields. Rejected Agentic because behavior checks argument passing correctness, not compliance reasoning quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run compliance invocation path with a test double client and assert outbound payload carries exact jurisdiction code and regulatory profile identifiers configured for the run.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly asserts correctness of compliance determination by a live AI reviewer; this depends on real model reasoning over legal/regulatory text. Mock responses would mask true decision errors. Rejected Workflow because this statement is single-reviewer correctness, not multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Send real contracts with known compliance outcomes to the live compliance checker and verify pass/fail plus requirement-level findings match expected regulatory ground truth.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is deterministic merge logic on provided reviewer outputs: preserve conflicting assessments and attribution. A local merge function/object test can catch regression without module/network/model dependencies. Rejected Integration because behavior text is scoped to merge-step rule given already-produced inputs, not cross-module dispatch/collection faults.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the merge function two conflicting findings for same clause plus source reviewer IDs; assert merged report contains both assessments and correct attribution metadata.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: This is a full pipeline with multiple AI reviewers in parallel plus merge, and correctness depends on sequential/parallel agentic interactions and handoff completeness. Single-step Agentic tests would miss cross-step loss/drop defects. Rejected lower levels because behavior explicitly requires end-to-end orchestration on a real contract across all three reviewers.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full production-like pipeline on real contracts: dispatch live extractor/risk/compliance reviewers, wait for all results, merge, then assert final report retains every reviewer finding with traceable attribution and no dropped clauses.
